O you enveloped in your cloak!Footnote-1
Arise, and warn,Footnote-2
and magnify the glory of your Lord,Footnote-3
and purify your robes,Footnote-4
and shun uncleanness,Footnote-5
and bestow not favour in order to seek from others a greater return,Footnote-6
and persevere for your Lord's sake.Footnote-7
When the Trumpet shall be sounded,
that will surely be a hard day,Footnote-8
not an easy day for the unbelievers.Footnote-9
Leave Me with himFootnote-10 whom I alone have created,Footnote-11
whom I have endowed with abundant riches,
and sons ever present with him,Footnote-12
and for whom I have smoothed the way (to power and riches),
and who still greedily desires that I should bestow upon him more.Footnote-13
By no means; he is stubbornly opposed to Our Signs.
I shall soon constrain him to a hard ascent.
He reflected and then hatched a scheme.
Ruin seize him, how did he hatch a scheme?
Again, ruin seize him, how did he hatch a scheme?
He looked (at others);
then frowned and scowled;
then he retreated and waxed proud,
and said: “This (Qur'an) is merely a sorcery of yore;
this is nothing but the word of a mere mortal!”Footnote-14
Him shall I soon roast in Hell.
And what do you know what Hell is?
It spares nothing; it leaves nothing intact;Footnote-15
it scorches (even) the skin.Footnote-16
Over it are nineteen keepers.

WeFootnote-17 have appointed none but angels as the keepers of the Fire,Footnote-18 and We have not made their number but as a trial for the unbelieversFootnote-19 so that those who have been endowed with the Book will be convincedFootnote-20 and the believers' faith will increase, Footnote-21 and neither those who have been endowed with the Book nor the believers will fall into any doubt. As for those in whose hearts there is a sicknessFootnote-22 as well as the unbelievers, they will say: “What did Allah aim at by this strange parable?”Footnote-23 Thus does Allah let whomsoever He pleases to go astray, and directs whomsoever He pleasesFootnote-24 to the Right Way. And none knows the hosts of your Lord but He.Footnote-25 (And Hell has only been mentioned here) that people may take heed.Footnote-26
Nay,Footnote-27 by the moon,
and by the night when it recedes,
and by the day when it dawns (with its radiance),
surely (Hell) is one of the greatest Signs,Footnote-28
a warning to humankind,
a warning to everyone of you whether he would like to come forward or lag behind.Footnote-29
Each one is a hostage to one's deeds,Footnote-30
save the People of the Right Hand
who shall be in the Gardens,Footnote-31 and shall ask
about the guilty ones:Footnote-32
“What drove you to Hell?”
They will answer: “We were not among those who observed Prayer,Footnote-33
and we did not feed the poor,Footnote-34
and we indulged in vain talk with those who indulged in vain talk,
and we gave the lie to the Day of Judgement
until the inevitable event overtook us.”Footnote-35
The intercession of the intercessors shall then be of no avail to them.Footnote-36
What is the matter with people that they are turning away from this Exhortation,
as though they were frightened wild asses,
fleeing from a lion?Footnote-37
No indeed; each one of them desires that open letters be sent to each of them.Footnote-38
No indeed; the truth is that they have no fear of the Hereafter.Footnote-39
Nay;Footnote-40 this is an Exhortation.
So, whoever wills may benefit from it.
But they will not benefit from it unless Allah Himself so wills.Footnote-41 He is worthy to be feared;Footnote-42 and He is worthy to forgive (those that fear Him).Footnote-43
